rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH A GAROFOLO et al VS. JON F DOYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Interim Arbitration Decision And Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH GAROFOLO Motion To Seal Interim Arbitration Decision And Award. Granted. The Interim Arbitration Decision and Award shall be sealed. Plaintiffs' proposed redacted Interim Arbitration Decision and Award shall be the public version. The Court finds that an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record, the overriding interest supports sealing the records, a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (CRC 2.550(d).) Defendants' opposition and request to redact more of the document does not establish the five factors set forth in CRC 2.550(d). Further, Plaintiffs establish in their reply brief that the majority of the information that Defendants seek to redact is already in the public record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA JOYA VS. LLOYD GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Neuropsychological Examination Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ESTATE OF LLOYD GONZALES-DECEDENT's Motion To Compel Neuropsychological Examination Of Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The examination is limited to 8 hours, excluding breaks. The examining doctor may inquire about plaintiff's medical history regarding part of her body that she does not claim were injured in the accident. The examining doctor may inquire about plaintiff's mental issues other than those plaintiff claims resulted from, or were exacerbated by, the incident. The examining doctor may inquire about plaintiff's relationship with others. The examining doctor must provide data related to any tests conducted on plaintiff. Defense counsel to email a proposed order to pcc@manningllp.com by 5pm today, cc'ing all counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL TORRES VS. AUSTIN EDWARDS HILLS 2004 TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT MANUEL TORRES DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. The Cross-Complaint does not allege a breach of the severance agreement, but rather a breach of an oral and/or implied agreement between the parties that if Plaintiff did not accept the severance offer made by Defendant, Plaintiff would return the money given to him by Defendant. (Cross-Compl. 3, 4.) Thus, there is no basis for the Court to consider the documents that are allegedly part of the severance offer. Additionally, the Cross-Complaint sufficiently alleges each element of the three remaining causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD OGUEJIOFOR VS. DOTUN OYENUGA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion And Motion To Strike Under Ccp Sec 425.16(Anti-Slapp)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD OGUEJIOFOR Notice Of Special Motion And Motion To Strike Under Ccp Sec 425.16(Anti-Slapp). Denied. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity¿If the court finds such a showing has been made¿it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) Cross-Defendant has not established that the cause of action arises from protected activity. The statements were not made in an "official proceeding." Although Labor Code sec. 1726 requires an "awarding body" to investigate potential prevailing wage violations, the Department of Industrial Relations defines "awarding body" as the "department, board, authority, officer or agent awarding a contract for public work." (Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. C, Printout from DIR webpage). The plain language of this definition establishes that the "awarding body" is the governmental entity that awards the contract. There is no language that indicates that this definition should be extended to include general contractors who hire subcontractors. Further, this case is distinguishable from Kibler v. Northern Inyo County Local Hospital Dist. (2006) 39 Cal.4th 192) as there is no indication that any investigation into payment of prevailing wages had commenced. Additionally, the statements do not relate to "to the substantive issues in the litigation and [were not] directed to persons having some interest in the litigation." (Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1266.) "Section 425.16 does not accord anti-SLAPP protection to suits arising from any act having any connection, however remote, with an official proceeding. The statements or writings in question must occur in connection with an issue under consideration or review in the proceeding." (Paul v. Friedman, 95 Cal. App. 4th 853, 866.) The underlying complaint in this matter asserts causes of action for wage and hour violations. The cross-complaint alleges that Cross-Defendant informed the general contractor that he was an independent contractor who anticipated filing a mechanic's lien on the project and that Cross-Complainant was hostile to the general contractor's employees. These statements do not relate to the substantive issues raised in the wage and hour causes of action. Because Cross-Defendant has not established that the cross-complaint arises out of protected activity, the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER LEX GEORGIOU et al VS. KEITH KINKEAD REILING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT KEITH REILING DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. First, the contract as alleged in the Complaint is not subject to the statute of frauds because, while unlikely, performance within one year cannot be deemed impossible. (See White Lighting Co. v. Wolfson (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343 fn. 2 ["there must not be the slightest possibility that it can be fully performed within one year"].) Further, whether performance in fact took longer than one year is irrelevant to determine whether the statute of fraud apples where, as here, the alleged terms of the contract do not indicate any such intent. (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675 fn. 18 ["The applicability of the statute of frauds must be analyzed prospectively, based on the intentions of the parties and the terms of the agreement at the time it is made."].) Second, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the elements for each cause of action. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 [To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged.].) Third, the Complaint clearly alleges that Plaintiffs entered into oral agreements with both Defendants, and allegations as to emails confirming those agreements is irrelevant and does not render the Complaint uncertain. Next, Defendants have cited no authority establishing that non-compliance with Business & Professions Code sections 7159.5 or 7164 render the alleged contracts unenforceable. Additionally, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the existence of a fiduciary duty based on the parties' relationship as partners and/or joint venturers. Further, the Complaint coherently pleads a breach of that duty. (See Compl. 67.) The Complaint also sufficiently alleges a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and is not a mere restatement of the claim for breach of contract. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to constructive fraud, accounting, and declaratory relief because Defendants' arguments lack merit. The Complaint plainly alleges facts sufficient to meet the heightened requirements for fraud claims (see Compl. 16, 20, 25, 27, 28, 78, 79), and each element of an accounting. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER LEX GEORGIOU et al VS. KEITH KINKEAD REILING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT THE HOFF HOUSE, LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. First, the contract as alleged in the Complaint is not subject to the statute of frauds because, while unlikely, performance within one year cannot be deemed impossible. (See White Lighting Co. v. Wolfson (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343 fn. 2 ["there must not be the slightest possibility that it can be fully performed within one year"].) (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675 fn. 18 ["The applicability of the statute of frauds must be analyzed prospectively, based on the intentions of the parties and the terms of the agreement at the time it is made."].) Second, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the elements for each cause of action. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 [To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged.].) Third, the Complaint clearly alleges that Plaintiffs entered into oral agreements with both Defendants, and allegations as to emails confirming those agreements is irrelevant and does not render the Complaint uncertain. Next, Defendants have cited no authority establishing that non-compliance with Business & Professions Code sections 7159.5 or 7164 render the alleged contracts unenforceable. Additionally, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the existence of a fiduciary duty based on the parties' relationship as partners and/or joint venturers. Further, the Complaint coherently pleads a breach of that duty. (See Compl. 67.) The Complaint also sufficiently alleges a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and is not a mere restatement of the claim for breach of contract. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to constructive fraud, accounting, and declaratory relief because Defendants' arguments lack merit. The Complaint plainly alleges facts sufficient to meet the heightened requirements for fraud claims (see Compl. 16, 20, 25, 27, 28, 78, 79), and each element of an accounting. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LAURIE POLK VS. COMMUNITY HOUSING PERTNERSHIP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Bifurcation Of Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT COMMUNITY HOUSING PARTNERSHIP's Motion For Bifurcation Of Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court did not receive courtesy copies of the motion or opposition papers. The hearing is continued to January 16, 2015. The parties shall provide courtesy copies of the moving and opposition papers to the Court by 9am on January 12, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY CHANDLER VS. STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: To Lift A Stay On Discovery And Postpone Anti-Slapp Motion Hearing (Ccp Sec 425.16(G)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 12, 2015, Line 14, PLAINTIFF GREGORY CHANDLER To Lift A Stay On Discovery And Postpone Anti-Slapp Motion Hearing (Ccp Sec 425.16(G). Denied. CCP § 425.16(g) stays discovery upon the filing of an Anti-SLAPP motion unless the Court finds "good cause" to lift the discovery stay. To satisfy the "good cause" requirement, the plaintiff must establish the viability of its claim by a prima facie showing. (Paterno v. Sup. Ct. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1349.) Plaintiff has not established good cause for lifting the discovery stay. He does not clearly identify what discovery is needed, nor does he explain how the evidence will establish a probability of prevailing on his causes of action. Additionally, the motion is also denied on procedural grounds. Pursuant to the Order Shortening Time issued on December 30, 2014, Plaintiff was required to serve Defendant with the motion on December 31, 2014. Plaintiff mailed the motion (which did not arrive to Defendant until January 2, 2015 due to the New Years holiday) and therefore was not in compliance with the Court's Order.
